description: The upper part of the body, containing the brain and the eyes, ears, nose, and mouth.
rationale: Combines 'hi' (breath/life) with 'mae' (a sound suggesting containment or a vessel). The meaning is 'the vessel of the breath/life/mind,' a fitting description for the head.

description: The part of a person's body at the end of the arm, used for holding, moving, touching, and feeling things.
rationale: The word combines 'pha' (the proximal particle, 'this') with 'le' (a flowing, connective sound). This constructs the meaning 'the thing here that connects/flows,' which beautifully describes the hand's role in interacting with the immediate environment.
This word is a nominalized form of the verb 'shipe' (to see/observe). The 'io' ending creates a noun that means 'the instrument of seeing.'
